title: calophyllum obliquinervium
intro: Calophyllum obliquinervium, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, is a type of evergreen tree belonging to the Calophyllaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains calophyllolide, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bark has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and powdered, then used in teas, infusions, or combined with other herbs to create tonics and remedies.
